item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an interesting event, but (1) I don't really understand how the translation to English occurred, (2) given the translation, the Foundation doesn't seem to have updated its description to reflect information from the contents of the transmission (which is convenient for narrative purposes but improbable in-world).

One solution to (2) might be to write up the SCP/description for the Keter anomaly, then present the translated transmission, and then present an updated and revised SCP/description that reclassifies the anomaly as Neutralized. Note that the revised SCP need not take the veracity of the transmission's contents for granted, but they should at least be noted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great stuff. Combining clinical descriptions with a dark, buried story is extremely hard to nail, but you did it perfectly. The fact that the log isn't written in conventional English and takes some figuring out is just icing on the cake, as it tells the story from an understandable but clearly otherworldly perspective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent SCP. Figuring out what's being documented, it was pretty incredible how quickly I went from totally confused to chilled and hugely impressed. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this. The log had me scratching my head trying to figure out what exactly was happening here. Once it dawned on me, I immediately hit +1. This is good stuff, and is an excellent lesson in making your readers feel as smart as the Foundation. Good stuff, Cyvstvi.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor scientific quibbles aside,1 I found the long-range radio wave log to be exceedingly chilling. I did not immediately understand what it was referring to at first, but the fridge logic jumped out at me upon reconsidering the article after the fact.

Footnotes
1. Which I will make sure to mention on #site19, because I find the science behind space to be fascinating, and I'd love to discuss it!

EDIT: After a brief discussion with Cyvstvi, my nitpick has been ironed out — there was a misunderstanding on my part.

Great work!

item.title: Auth Post
item.content:encoded:
What's this? A scip by Cyvstvi?

Hell yeah it is. Thanks to cybersqyd, AlanDaris, and MalyceGraves for their monumental critique on this piece.

Ad Astra.
